title: ALIGN Act
plain_summary: The ALIGN Act is a law that helps businesses save money on their taxes. When companies buy equipment or property for their business, they can subtract the full cost from their taxes right away instead of waiting several years to get the full tax break. This tax benefit was going to end soon, but the new law makes it last forever. This means businesses can keep getting this tax break every year when they buy new equipment.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Accelerate Long-term Investment Growth Now Act or the ALIGN Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Permanent Full Expensing for Qualified Property
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to make a business tax break permanent. Under current law, businesses can deduct the full cost of certain equipment and property in the year they buy it, instead of spreading the deduction over many years. This tax break was set to expire, but this law makes it permanent at one hundred percent. The change applies to property that businesses put into use after September twenty-seventh, two thousand seventeen. For plants that are planted or grafted, the same rule applies. The section also makes technical changes to other parts of the tax code to remove the expiration dates and references to the temporary nature of this tax break. These changes take effect as if they were part of the original tax reform law passed in two thousand seventeen.
